assertions.text: The vehicle was traveling east at about 85 km/h prior to drifting off the road.
The driver applied the brakes well before leaving the roadway.
Braking occurred before the vehicle left the roadway.
The front of the car struck a bridge abutment.
There was a single impact in which the front of the car struck the bridge abutment.
The front of the car struck the bridge abutment (implying a collision of at least moderate severity).

verdicts.evidence: The telemetry shows the vehicle speed samples rising from [77.0 km/h at t=-5.0 s](T3) to a peak of [90.0 km/h] and the last pre-impact sample at [87.0 km/h at t=0.0 s](T2), so the claim of ~[85 km/h] eastbound is not supported by sensor direction (direction/east not in sensors) and the numeric value is slightly higher; heading/east is unverifiable from available data. The numeric part (85 km/h) is within ~3 km/h of recorded [87.0 km/h](T2) so speed magnitude is close, but I must note that heading (east) is unverifiable.

Brake channel samples show the brake was [0 (off)] in all pre-crash samples including the last at [t=0.0 s](T3), and compute_stats reports [fraction_on: 0.0](T2), so the claim that the driver 'stood on the brakes well before' is contradicted by the telemetry showing no brake application.

Because the brake channel is [off for all samples pre-crash](T3) and compute_stats shows [fraction_on: 0.0](T2), there is no telemetry evidence that braking occurred before leaving the roadway; the data contradicts the stated sequence.

IMU/EDR channels include crash delta-Vs ([max_delta_v_long -87.101 km/h at t=90 ms](T1) and [max_delta_v_lat -10.948 km/h at t=40 ms](T1)), indicating a large longitudinal impact, but the sensors do not record environment objects. The specific object 'bridge abutment' is not observable in the vehicle telemetry and is therefore unverifiable.

The EDR summary reports [num_events_recorded: 1](T1) which indicates one recorded crash event in the EDR, but telemetry alone cannot confirm whether that corresponds to a single distinct contact with the abutment versus multiple contacts within the event; thus the precise count/description of 'single impact front struck abutment' is unverifiable from sensors alone.

The crash pulse shows a large longitudinal delta-V reported as [max_delta_v_long = -87.101 km/h at 90 ms](T1), which indicates a severe forward impact consistent with a front strike; the magnitude supports the claim of at least moderate severity.

result.rationale: Speed magnitude matches telemetry closely but heading is not recorded; brake channel shows no pre-impact braking so braking assertions are contradicted; object struck and impact count are not observable from vehicle sensors; crash pulse magnitude supports a moderate-to-severe front impact.

result.time_zero: impact (t=0); pre-crash negative, crash pulse positive

channels.name: delta_v_long
channels.desc: Cumulative longitudinal delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

channels.name: delta_v_lat
channels.desc: Cumulative lateral delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

result.notes: NHTSA CISS EDR data: sparse pre-crash series (~2 Hz, t<0) plus a crash-pulse delta-V curve (t>=0, ~10 ms steps). t=0 is the impact.

delta_v_long.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse

delta_v_lat.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse

result.downsampling: per-bucket sample with the largest |deviation from channel median| is kept
